name: tmp_cwd_domain_gap_closed
tags: integration test cwd tmp domain gap memory injection test_server_api
body: Found and closed a real gap: tests posting cwd="/tmp" (test_server_api.py, test_hooks_lc.py, etc.) previously hit an unmapped cwd — cwd_domains.json had no "/tmp" entry, so those hook calls silently fell into the cwd_unmapped/no-domain path instead of exercising real domain-scoped memory injection. Fixed by adding "/tmp": "test" to cwd_domains.json, seeding two fixed reference memories (test-fixture-alpha/beta) under domain=test in MEMORY.sqlite, and adding TestTmpCwdFixedMemories in tests/test_server_api.py asserting both names appear in the UserPromptSubmit response's additionalSystemPrompt. Why: confirms cwd→domain→memory-injection pipeline actually resolves for the exact cwd value integration tests use, not just full domains like claude-hooks/vault/astrology. How to apply: when adding new integration tests that pass a synthetic cwd, check it resolves to a real (or intentionally test-only) domain in cwd_domains.json rather than silently no-op'ing.
